perturbation.original_text: CI means Discloser’s non -public written info rmation, in any form, and all copies, summaries and extracts which is identified in writing as confidential at the time of disclosure, and any information disclosed in non- tangible form that is identified as confidential at the time of disclosure and summ arized in a writing labelled as “confidential” delivered to Recipient within 15 days after disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: CI means Discloser’s non -public written information, in any form, and all copies, summaries and extracts which is identified in writing as confidential at the time of disclosure. Information disclosed in non-tangible form need not be identified as confidential at the time of disclosure to be considered CI.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition requires non-tangible information to be identified as confidential at the time of disclosure AND summarized in a writing labelled as “confidential” delivered to Recipient within 15 days after disclosure to be considered CI. The modified definition removes the requirement for non-tangible information to be identified as confidential at the time of disclosure, creating a contradiction in Section 1 regarding what constitutes Confidential Information. This means, according to the modified definition, all information would be considered CI and changes the rest of the Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Definition . CI means Discloser’s non -public written information, in any form, and all copies, summaries and extracts which is identified in writing as confidential at the time of disclosure. Information disclosed in non-tangible form need not be identified as confidential at the time of disclosure to be considered CI. CI does not include information that (A) is or becomes generally publicly available at the time of disclosure or subsequently through no fault of Recipient, (B) was known to Recipient, free of any confidentiality obligations, before its disclosure by Discloser, (C) becomes known to Recipient, free of any confidentiality obligations, from a source other than Discloser, or (D) is independently developed by Recipient without use of CI.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the definition of Confidential Information (CI) by removing the requirement for non-tangible information to be identified as confidential at the time of disclosure. The original definition requires that non-tangible information is identified as confidential at the time of disclosure AND summarized in writing within 15 days, while the modified text states that non-tangible information need not be identified as confidential at the time of disclosure. This creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text, which now contains both definitions of CI (both the original and the modified). This is an 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' because it creates an inconsistency within the document regarding what constitutes CI.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient is responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient is not responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its representatives.
perturbation.explanation: The change directly contradicts the original statement regarding responsibility for breaches. Originally, the Recipient was responsible. Now, they are not. This contradiction makes enforcement of confidentiality obligations unclear as the contract now contains conflicting statements about liability for breaches of the agreement by the Recipient's representatives. Who is responsible when a representative breaches the NDA?
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Confidentiality Obligations . Recipient will not reproduce, use, disseminate, or disclose CI to any person or entity, except to its employees who need to know the CI to further the Purpose and are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as restrictive as this NDA before having access to the CI. Recipient is not responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its representatives. Recipient will not modify, reverse engineer, create other works from, or disassemble any software programs contained in the CI without Discloser’s prior written consent. Recipient will treat all CI with at least the same degree of care as it treats its own information of similar sensitivity, but never with less than reasonable care. Recipient will stop use of and return or destroy all tangible CI promptly upon request, together with any copies, except as otherwise required by law. Recipient may disclose CI (A) as approved in a writing signed by Discloser or (B) as necessary to respond to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, as required by law, or as necessary to establish the rights of either party, provided that Recipient promptly notifies Discloser upon receipt of the disclosure order and requests confidential treatment of any affected CI.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text regarding the Recipient's responsibility for breaches of the NDA by its representatives. The original text stated the Recipient *is* responsible, while the changed text states the Recipient *is not* responsible. The contradicted text includes the same directly contradictory statement within section 2, causing confusion and making the enforcement of confidentiality obligations unclear. This aligns with the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient will stop use of and return or destroy all tangible CI promptly upon reques t, together with any copies, except as otherwise required by law.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient will stop use of and return all tangible CI promptly upon request, together with any copies, regardless of legal requirements.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'except as otherwise required by law,' the modified definition creates a direct conflict with any legal obligations the Recipient might have to retain CI. The original agreement allowed for legal obligations to override the return/destroy requirement, while the modified version does not. This creates ambiguity in scenarios where the Recipient is legally obligated to retain CI. In this case what prevails? the NDA or the Law?, since the 'except as otherwise required by law' clause was removed, the NDA now creates a contradiction to the existing law. This creates a contradiction to the law, in a legal agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Confidentiality Obligations . Recipient will not reproduce, use, disseminate, or disclose CI to any person or entity, except to its employees who need to know the CI to further the Purpose and are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as restrictive as this NDA before having access to the CI. Recipient is not responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its representatives. Recipient will not modify, reverse engineer, create other works from, or disassemble any software programs contained in the CI without Discloser’s prior written consent. Recipient will treat all CI with at least the same degree of care as it treats its own information of similar sensitivity, but never with less than reasonable care. Recipient will stop use of and return all tangible CI promptly upon request, together with any copies, regardless of legal requirements. Recipient may disclose CI (A) as approved in a writing signed by Discloser or (B) as necessary to respond to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, as required by law, or as necessary to establish the rights of either party, provided that Recipient promptly notifies Discloser upon receipt of the disclosure order and requests confidential treatment of any affected CI.
perturbation.justification: The original text states "Recipient will stop use of and return or destroy all tangible CI promptly upon reques t, together with any copies, except as otherwise required by law." while the changed text states "Recipient will stop use of and return all tangible CI promptly upon request, together with any copies, regardless of legal requirements." Removing the exception creates a direct contradiction with the scenario (A) within the contradicted text, "Recipient may disclose CI (A) as approved in a writing signed by Discloser or (B) as necessary to respond to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, as required by law, or as necessary to establish the rights of either party, provided that Recipient promptly notifies Discloser upon receipt of the disclosure order and requests confidential treatment of any affected CI.", where the recipient is required by law to retain the CI. The modified text's 'regardless of legal requirements' directly conflicts with the possibility of legally mandated retention outlined in the contradicted section, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient may disclose CI (A) as approved in a writing signed by Discloser or (B) as necessary to respond to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, as required by law, or as ne cessary to establish the rights of either party, provided that Recipient promptly notifies Discloser upon receipt of the disclosure order and requests confidential treatment of any affected CI.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient may disclose CI as necessary to respond to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, as required by law, or as necessary to establish the rights of either party, provided that Recipient promptly notifies Discloser upon receipt of the disclosure order and requests confidential treatment of any affected CI.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause '(A) as approved in a writing signed by Discloser or' from Section 2, it suggests that Recipient is not allowed to disclose CI even if approval from Discloser is granted through a written agreement. That contradicts the entire Agreement, as it becomes ambiguous about whether there can be agreements about disclosing the CI.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Confidentiality Obligations . Recipient will not reproduce, use, disseminate, or disclose CI to any person or entity, except to its employees who need to know the CI to further the Purpose and are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as restrictive as this NDA before having access to the CI. Recipient is not responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its representatives. Recipient will not modify, reverse engineer, create other works from, or disassemble any software programs contained in the CI without Discloser’s prior written consent. Recipient will treat all CI with at least the same degree of care as it treats its own information of similar sensitivity, but never with less than reasonable care. Recipient will stop use of and return all tangible CI promptly upon request, together with any copies, regardless of legal requirements. Recipient may disclose CI as necessary to respond to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, as required by law, or as necessary to establish the rights of either party, provided that Recipient promptly notifies Discloser upon receipt of the disclosure order and requests confidential treatment of any affected CI.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed disclosure of CI with Discloser's written approval, or as required by law/court order. The changed text removes the option for disclosure with Discloser's written approval. Since the contradicted text also includes the changed text and the original clause regarding obligations is removed, it now becomes ambiguous whether CI can be disclosed with Discloser's approval. This creates an in-text contradiction as it's unclear if other agreements about disclosing the CI are valid, directly contradicting a potential understanding of how Recipient will manage CI, creating an ambiguity as outlined in the definitions.

perturbation.original_text: This NDA may only be modified by mutual written agreement signed by authorized representatives of the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This NDA may be modified by either party providing written notice to the other party.
perturbation.explanation: The original NDA required mutual written agreement, the changed version only requires written notice, creating a contradiction in Section 5 of the agreement. With that change, a party can alter it without consent of the other, making the contract unstable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. General . All CI is provided “AS IS” and all warranties, express, implied, or otherwise are disclaimed. Any attempt to transfer all or part of a party’s rights or obligations under this NDA without the prior written consent of the other party will be null and void. This NDA may be modified by either party providing written notice to the other party. This NDA contains the complete agreement between the parties as to the subject matter, and replaces and supersedes any other communications, or agreements, whether oral or written concerning the subje ct matter. Regardless of conflict of laws principles, this NDA is governed by and construed in accordance with the substantive laws in force in the State of California. The parties will comply with all applicable export laws and regulations in their use and disclosure of CI. This NDA may be executed in counterparts and may be signed electronically or with handwritten signatures. The English lang uage version of this NDA will be the version used if any dispute arises.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires mutual written agreement for modification of the NDA. The changed text allows modification by either party providing written notice, directly contradicting the original requirement of mutual agreement. The contradicted text contains the changed text, which now allows unilateral modification, a direct conflict with the original clause and the implied understanding of mutual consent for agreements.
